props.message:
The hearth is cold.

You can still smell pine-smoke if you stand close enough. Or you think you can. Sometimes the wanting is enough.

Two chairs by the cold hearth.

The chair where she sat. The chair where he sat. They are turned a little toward each other still.

The garden has gone to seed.

What the Wyrdkin planted has spread, and softened, and become the kind of green that does not need keeping.

An old letter, folded into the wall:

"My own one — I will be back before the snow that we do not have. The road is long but the road is the road. Keep my chair warm. Walk well."

A recipe scratched into the table-edge:

"Sweetfern pie. Three handfuls of sweetfern. Two of root-honey. One of patience. The Wyrdkin do not make this any more."

A small memorial stone outside the back door:

"Two names, kept close.
Walk well, both of you."

props.open_message: A Heart Container — the line endures.

root.speaker: Old Wyrdkin Grandmother
root.text: Oh — oh, who is that? The light is wrong, child. Stand a little closer. My eyes are a memory of eyes.

name.speaker: Old Wyrdkin Grandmother
name.text: Tux. Tux. The name is fond on the tongue. I knew a Tux once — small as a kettle, brave as a kettle on the boil. Are you that one?

warm.speaker: Old Wyrdkin Grandmother
warm.text: Then come a little closer. The hearth is cold but the room remembers being warm. Sit a moment with an old ghost. Tell me — do you carry a sword?

look.speaker: Old Wyrdkin Grandmother
look.text: Bone-and-wood. He carried bone-and-wood, my own one did. Let me see your face properly, child. Lirien's breath —

props.open_message: Among the run-wild herbs of the back garden — the wyrdkin who lived here taught Tux the names of every leaf. He remembers them now.

load_zones.prompt: [E] Down the hall of carved tablets
